_comment: SC-001a / SC-002 baseline — manually captured, never auto-updated. Re-capture requires a reviewed commit.

reference_machine.cpu: x86_64 GitHub Actions hosted runner (ubuntu-latest, 4-core). See git log for exact capture commit.

lint_10kb._note: Values are Criterion 95% confidence-interval bounds captured on a GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest hosted runner (two independent runs observed [534–535µs, 537–540µs] upper-CI). The baseline is now CI-reproducible, so the +10% percentage gate against `upper_ci_us` is re-enabled (threshold ~596µs). The `drift_alert_upper_ci_us` workaround used in the WSL2-captured baseline is no longer needed and has been removed. The SC-001 16ms absolute target remains the load-bearing constitution gate.

decoder_10kb_one_mangled_region._note: Engine::lint with `with_deep_scan()` on a 10KB document containing exactly one mangled portion (`(SERCET//NF)`) plus normal valid markings + prose. `SERCET` is the deliberate misspelling the SC-002 bench feeds the engine to force the decoder to fire — it is NOT a typo to be corrected. Captured on a GitHub Actions ubuntu-latest hosted runner (two independent runs observed [668–673µs, 679–680µs] upper-CI). The baseline is now CI-reproducible, so the +10% percentage gate against `upper_ci_us` is re-enabled (threshold ~750µs). The `drift_alert_upper_ci_us` workaround used in the WSL2-captured baseline is no longer needed and has been removed. The SC-002 18ms absolute target remains the load-bearing constitution gate.

lint_scaling._note: SC-005 linear-scaling gate. R² is the coefficient of determination from an ordinary-least-squares fit of (input_size_bytes, mean_time_µs) across the input-size sweep in `crates/engine/benches/linear_scaling.rs` (currently 1KB → 100KB across seven points). 0.9 is the floor — substantially-better R² is normal for the strict path because per-document work is dominated by linear scanner + parser passes; a value below 0.9 usually indicates either super-linear behavior (a new quadratic somewhere) or pathological noise from the runner. Enforced by `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_linear_scaling`. Choice of input-size scaling vs. worker-count scaling is documented in the script header.

deadline_overhead._note: Spec 005 T018/T019. Compares `deadline_overhead_baseline` (Engine::lint, no deadline) against `deadline_overhead_with_deadline` (Engine::lint_with_options with a 1-hour deadline that never trips). The ratio is computed from Criterion's mean (middle CI value), not the upper-CI bound — clock jitter inflates CI width and would convert noise into false positives on a tight ratio gate. The gate is set at 10% (permissive starting point) rather than the 2% design target from `specs/005-engine-deadlines/spec.md` because the per-candidate Instant::now() cost varies dramatically by host: native Linux x86 vDSO clock_gettime is ~10–30ns and the spec's ≤2% target is reachable, but WSL2's virtualized HV clock is ~500–700ns and observed end-to-end overhead is 7–12% on the same fixture. Tightening this gate to 2% is a follow-up once a CI-runner baseline is captured (mirrors the `MARQUE_BENCH_SKIP_REGRESSION` pattern for the +10% absolute regression gate). Enforced by `scripts/bench-check.sh::check_deadline_overhead`.
